_comment: Tecnologías genéricas de TI4 (Base + PoK), SIN unit upgrades (esas están en unitUpgrades.json). Fuente: TI4 Living Rules Reference (FFG), Twilight Imperium Wiki, ti4lookup.com.

_schema.name: nombre oficial en inglés
_schema.prerequisites: array de colores requeridos (pueden repetirse). [] = sin prerrequisitos
_schema.exhaustable: true si la carta se puede agotar para activar un efecto

generic.name: Neural Motivator
generic.effect: During the status phase, draw 2 action cards instead of 1.

generic.name: Psychoarchaeology
generic.effect: You can use technology specialties on planets you control without exhausting them, even if those planets are exhausted. During the action phase, you can exhaust planets you control that have technology specialties to gain 1 trade good.

generic.name: Dacxive Animators
generic.effect: After you win a ground combat, you may place 1 infantry from your reinforcements on that planet.

generic.name: Bio-Stims
generic.effect: You may exhaust this card at the start of a player's turn; if you do, that player may exhaust or ready 1 of their planets.

generic.name: Hyper Metabolism
generic.effect: During the status phase, gain 3 command tokens instead of 2.

generic.name: X-89 Bacterial Weapon
generic.effect: ACTION: Exhaust this card and choose 1 planet in a system that contains 1 or more of your ships that have BOMBARDMENT; destroy all infantry on that planet.

generic.name: Plasma Scoring
generic.effect: When 1 or more of your units use BOMBARDMENT or SPACE CANNON, 1 of those units may roll 1 additional die.

generic.name: AI Development Algorithm
generic.effect: When you research a unit upgrade technology, you may exhaust this card to ignore all of that technology's prerequisites.

generic.name: Magen Defense Grid
generic.effect: BEFORE COMBAT: You may exhaust this card; if you do, apply +1 to the result of each of your units' combat rolls during this combat round.

generic.name: Self Assembly Routines
generic.effect: After you produce a mech, you may exhaust this card to produce 1 additional mech without paying its cost.

generic.name: Duranium Armor
generic.effect: During each space combat round, after you assign hits to your units, repair 1 of your units that has SUSTAIN DAMAGE.

generic.name: Assault Cannon
generic.effect: At the start of a space combat in a system that contains 3 or more of your non-fighter ships, your opponent must destroy 1 of their non-fighter ships.

generic.name: Antimass Deflectors
generic.effect: Your ships can move into and through asteroid fields. When other players' units use SPACE CANNON against your units, apply -1 to the result of each die roll.

generic.name: Dark Energy Tap
generic.effect: After you perform a tactical action in a system that contains a frontier token, if you have 1 or more ships in that system, explore that token.

generic.name: Gravity Drive
generic.effect: After you activate a system, you may exhaust this card to apply +1 to the move value of 1 of your ships during this tactical action.

generic.name: Sling Relay
generic.effect: When you activate a system, you may exhaust this card to explore 1 planet in that system which contains 1 or more of your units.

generic.name: Fleet Logistics
generic.effect: During each of your turns of the action phase, you may perform 2 actions instead of 1.

generic.name: Light/Wave Deflector
generic.effect: Your ships can move through systems that contain other players' ships.

generic.name: Sarween Tools
generic.effect: When 1 or more of your units use PRODUCTION, reduce the combined cost of the produced units by 1.

generic.name: Scanlink Drone Network
generic.effect: When you activate a system, you may explore 1 planet in that system which contains 1 or more of your units.

generic.name: Graviton Laser System
generic.effect: You may exhaust this card before a player assigns hits to their units; if you do, all hits produced must be assigned to non-fighter ships if able.

generic.name: Predictive Intelligence
generic.effect: You may exhaust this card at the end of your turn; if you do, remove all of your command tokens from the board and return them to your reinforcements.

generic.name: Transit Diodes
generic.effect: You may exhaust this card at the start of your turn; if you do, remove up to 4 of your ground forces from the game board and place them on 1 or more planets you control.

generic.name: Integrated Economy
generic.effect: After you gain control of a planet, you may produce any number of units on that planet that have a combined cost equal to or less than that planet's resource value.
